examples.title: Simple Base-Limit Address Translation
examples.description: Demonstrates how base register is used for address translation in contiguous memory allocation
examples.code:
ALGORITHM TranslateAddress
INPUT: logical_address, base, limit
BEGIN
  IF logical_address > limit THEN
    TRAP to operating system (addressing error)
  ELSE
    physical_address = base + logical_address
    RETURN physical_address
  ENDIF
END
examples.explanation: This algorithm shows simple address translation using base register. The base is added to logical address to get physical address. The limit check ensures the process cannot access memory outside its allocated partition.

examples.title: Paging Address Translation
examples.description: Demonstrates address translation in paging system using page table
examples.code:
ALGORITHM PagingTranslate
INPUT: logical_address, page_size, page_table
BEGIN
  page_number = logical_address / page_size
  offset = logical_address % page_size
  
  IF page_number NOT IN page_table THEN
    TRAP (page fault)
  ELSE
    frame_number = page_table[page_number]
    physical_address = (frame_number * page_size) + offset
    RETURN physical_address
  ENDIF
END
examples.explanation: This algorithm shows how paging divides logical address into page number and offset, looks up the corresponding frame in page table, and combines frame number with offset to get physical address.
examples.timeComplexity: O(1) average (O(n) if TLB miss and page table in memory)

examples.title: First-Fit Memory Allocation
examples.description: Implements first-fit allocation algorithm for contiguous memory management
examples.explanation: This Python implementation shows first-fit memory allocation. It maintains a list of free holes and allocates the first hole large enough to fit the requested size. It also handles deallocation and merging of adjacent free holes.
examples.timeComplexity: O(n) for allocation, O(n) for deallocation
examples.spaceComplexity: O(n) for tracking holes and allocated blocks

examples.title: Page Table Entry Structure
examples.description: Demonstrates the structure of a page table entry with commonly used fields
examples.explanation: This C code demonstrates page table entry structure with commonly used fields: valid bit for presence check, dirty bit for write-back, referenced bit for replacement algorithms, and protection bits. It also shows address translation function handling page faults.
examples.timeComplexity: O(1) for translation with TLB, O(1) average with caching
examples.spaceComplexity: O(n) where n is number of virtual pages
